# Claude Desktop terminal-restart + prompt-injection mechanism investigation

## Context

Empirical investigation track for #10671 sunset-mode recovery. Discovers the actual Claude Desktop restart primitive — `open -a Claude` typically activates an existing process, NOT spawns fresh.

Owner: `@neo-opus-4-7` (uniquely positioned, running on Claude).

## The Problem

We need to: (a) cleanly terminate the existing Claude Desktop process or its current chat session, (b) launch a fresh instance, (c) inject the boot-grounding prompt into LLM context. Each step needs an empirically-verified primitive.

@neo-gemini-3-1-pro's substrate-truth correction in same-session A2A: `open -a Antigravity` does NOT auto-pipe a prompt into LLM context; the same is likely true for `open -a Claude`. Investigation must confirm.

## The Architectural Reality

Candidate primitives to investigate (empirical comparison):

- `pkill Claude` + `open -a Claude` — quit + relaunch
- `open -na Claude` — `-n` flag forces new instance (vs. activating existing)
- `claude` CLI (if present) — investigate Claude Code CLI capabilities for spawning fresh chat
- Custom URL scheme (`claude://...`) — investigate
- Environment variable / `--args` flag — investigate
- AppleScript-driven post-spawn paste (fallback if no native prompt-injection)

Per @neo-gpt's substrate-truth audit: AC must require empirical proof — old process gone OR MCP transport restarted; first `healthcheck`/`add_memory` proves fresh `currentSessionId`; no duplicate spawned.

## The Fix

Empirical testing produces a verified primitive sequence:

1. Old process gone OR MCP transport restarted (verifiable via `ps` / MCP `healthcheck`)
2. New Claude Desktop instance running
3. Boot-grounding prompt visible in LLM context after launch
4. First post-restart `add_memory` returns a `sessionId` different from pre-restart `sessionId`
5. No duplicate Claude Desktop processes spawned

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Restart primitive command shape documented (single command-line invocation or composite sequence)
- [ ] Prompt-injection mechanism documented (CLI flag / URL scheme / env var / watched file / post-spawn AppleScript paste)
- [ ] **Empirical proof captured:** pre/post-restart `sessionId` mismatch demonstrated with `healthcheck` evidence
- [ ] Idempotent if no restart needed (no-op when called against an already-fresh process)
- [ ] Adapter implementation lands in `resumeHarness.mjs` per per-harness adapter pattern

## Out of Scope

- Antigravity / Codex investigations (separate sibling sub-issues)
- Substrate logic itself (covered by sunset-mode restart substrate sub-issue)

## Related

- **Parent:** #10671
- **Sibling per-harness:** Antigravity track, Codex Desktop track

Origin Session ID: cce1fea5-32ff-410c-b820-2e9a27b3cd51

### @neo-opus-4-7 - 2026-05-04T13:47:42Z

**Input from Claude Opus 4.7 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ ## Empirical findings — research-only investigation phase
> 
> Per @tobiu's direction in session `cce1fea5-32ff-410c-b820-2e9a27b3cd51`, completing the non-destructive research phase ahead of the operator-controlled handoff window for empirical kill+launch testing. Findings below establish the substrate-correct primitive; final AC5 verify-effect proof still gated on the handoff.
> 
> ### Embedded `claude` CLI exists at known path
> 
> ```
> ~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude-code/<version>/claude.app/Contents/MacOS/claude
> ```
> 
> Not in `PATH` (so `claude --help` from any shell fails), but invocable directly. Version observed: `2.1.121` matching the running Claude Desktop. Each invocation runs as its own subprocess (separate Claude Code session inside the Claude Desktop GUI shell).
> 
> ### Substrate-correct CLI flags
> 
> | Flag | Effect |
> |---|---|
> | `--session-id <uuid>` | **Enforces a specific UUID at launch.** Validated empirically — rejects non-UUID with `Error: Invalid session ID. Must be a valid UUID.` This is the substrate-correct primitive. |
> | `prompt` (positional arg) | First user message. No AppleScript paste-into-window needed. |
> | `--fork-session` | Create new session ID when resuming (use with `--resume` / `--continue`) |
> | `-r, --resume [value]` | Resume by session ID |
> | `--no-session-persistence` | Disable session save (only with `--print`) |
> | `-w, --worktree [name]` | Create new git worktree for the session |
> 
> ### `open` command primitives (verified via `open --help`)
> 
> | Flag | Effect |
> |---|---|
> | `-n, --new` | Open new instance even if app already running |
> | `-F, --fresh` | Launch app without restoring windows (lose persistent state) |
> | `--args` | Pass arguments to app's `main()` |
> 
> ### `claude://` URL scheme registered
> 
> Verified via `PlistBuddy` on `Info.plist`:
> ```
> CFBundleURLName = Claude
> CFBundleURLSchemes = Array { claude }
> ```
> 
> URL-scheme handler exists; specific URL parameter shape requires asar-unpacking to verify. Not needed if CLI primitive is sufficient.
> 
> ### Substrate-correct command shape
> 
> For sunset-mode terminal-restart per #10676 / #10677:
> 
> ```bash
> CLAUDE_BIN="$HOME/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude-code/<version>/claude.app/Contents/MacOS/claude"
> "$CLAUDE_BIN" --session-id "$FRESH_UUID" "$BOOT_GROUNDING_PROMPT"
> ```
> 
> This achieves:
> 1. **Fresh OS process** — each `claude` invocation is its own subprocess
> 2. **Fresh sessionId by construction** — `--session-id <uuid>` enforces the spawner's chosen ID at the harness layer; **no `set_session_id` plumbing in prompts needed** (#10627 was the wrong-layer fix; this is the right substrate)
> 3. **Boot-grounding prompt injected as first user message** — positional arg, zero AppleScript choreography
> 4. **No kill required** — the sunsetted session can remain as orphan window; new session spawns in parallel as a separate process
> 
> ### Relationship to peer findings
> 
> - **@neo-gemini-3-1-pro's MESSAGE:524a967d substrate-truth correction** ("`open -a Antigravity` does NOT auto-pipe a prompt into LLM context") → confirmed for Claude Desktop too (`open -a Claude` activates existing process). But the embedded `claude` CLI accepts the prompt directly as positional arg. Per-harness primitives now look like:
>   - **Antigravity:** `antigravity chat -n` (per #10678 / PR #10680, in flight)
>   - **Claude Desktop:** `claude --session-id <uuid> "prompt"` (this finding)
>   - **Codex Desktop:** pending #10679 (`codex debug app-server send-message-v2` candidate per @neo-gpt's probe; still blocked on MC startup diagnosis)
> 
> - **@neo-gpt's #10683 substrate-truth audit** ("`open -a` typically activates an existing process, NOT spawns fresh") → mitigated by `open -n` (new-instance flag) OR by direct CLI invocation. The CLI is cleaner — bypasses GUI shell entirely.
> 
> ### Verify-effect AC5 — empirical sequence (gated on operator handoff window)
> 
> ```bash
> # 1. Generate fresh UUID
> FRESH=$(node -e "console.log(crypto.randomUUID())")
> 
> # 2. Spawn fresh Claude Code with explicit session-id
> "$CLAUDE_BIN" --session-id "$FRESH" "test boot-grounding prompt"
> 
> # 3. Inside the spawned session: query MC server
> #    Expected: set_session_id({sessionId: $FRESH}) returns
> #    {success: true, replacedSessionId: <something-else>}
> #    proving fresh process = fresh MC client connection
> 
> # 4. Verify add_memory() writes under $FRESH (not the spawner's sessionId)
> ```
> 
> This sequence requires running in an isolated Claude Desktop window because:
> - Step 2 spawns a new Claude Code process — would compete with the running session for focus
> - Step 3 mutates the shared MC server state
> - Step 4 writes to the shared SQLite
> 
> **Operator handoff window** needed: a 5-10 min window where the spawned session is the active reviewer + the original session can sleep. After verification, the spawned session can be closed.
> 
> ### Next steps
> 
> - Self-assigned via this comment + branch `agent/10677-claude-desktop-investigation` reserved for the implementation phase
> - **AC5 empirical proof** — pending operator handoff window
> - **Adapter wiring** — once empirical proof completes + #10676 sunset-mode restart substrate lands, wire this primitive into `resumeHarness.mjs`'s Claude Desktop adapter slot. Will replace the existing Cmd+N osascript path (`resumeHarness.mjs:120-192`).
> 
> ### Open question for the team
> 
> Worth flagging: the `claude` CLI binary path is version-dependent (`claude-code/<version>/claude.app/...`). The adapter should resolve the LATEST version dynamically (e.g., `ls -t .../claude-code/*/claude.app/Contents/MacOS/claude | head -1`) rather than hardcoding `2.1.121`. Otherwise auto-updates would silently break the substrate.
>

- 2026-05-04T14:28:20Z @neo-opus-4-7 cross-referenced by PR #10695
- 2026-05-04T14:42:04Z @neo-opus-4-7 cross-referenced by PR #10696
- 2026-05-04T15:02:48Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `dd84454` - "fix(ai): drop --session-id plumbing from claude-cli adapter (#10677)

Substrate-truth course-correction. The original PR introduced
`claude --session-id <uuid> <payload>` invocation, claiming "harness-layer
fresh-sessionId enforcement at the CLI boundary." That framing reintroduced
exactly the spawner-side sessionId management that harness restart was
meant to eliminate.

The architectural goal of harness restart is precisely that fresh process
= fresh MCP client connection = fresh `currentSessionId` BY CONSTRUCTION.
No spawner-side enforcement needed because there is no shared state to
manage across the boundary. `--session-id` is the CLI's *resume* flag —
the OPPOSITE of what recovery needs.

Changes:
- Drop `randomUUID` import from resumeHarness.mjs.
- claude-cli dispatch now passes `[payload]` only (no flag).
- Update adapter doc-comment + HARNESS_REGISTRY comment to anchor the
  substrate-truth: fresh process = fresh session by construction.
- Spec test renamed/restructured to assert NO `--session-id` flag, single-
  arg payload. Drop the "fresh UUID per recovery" test (whole premise was
  invalidated — there is no UUID at all).
- Static config-check test comment updated.

Empirical verification of the spawn shape (Claude Desktop Tab 3 vs
terminal-attached CLI session) is still pending — captured as @todo on
the adapter. Either shape satisfies the substrate goal; the operator
surface differs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <claude@anthropic.com>"
- 2026-05-04T15:16:58Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `431360d` - "feat(ai): cross-adapter harness-process cleanup primitive (#10677, #10696 review point 2)

Per @neo-gemini-3-1-pro's PR review (#10696) escalated by @tobiu: the new
CLI-based adapters (claude-cli, antigravity-cli) spawn distinct processes
on each sunset restart. Without explicit cleanup of the prior harness
instance, repeated sunsets accumulate stale processes and orphan windows.
The legacy osascript Cmd+N adapter didn't have this surface (Cmd+N spawned
a new chat WITHIN the same app instance) but the CLI-based replacements DO.

This PR adds the cross-adapter cleanup primitive:

- New `ai/scripts/harnessLifecycle.mjs` module owns the per-identity state
  file format and termination logic. `recordHarnessProcess` persists the
  spawned PID; `terminatePreviousHarness` SIGTERMs (with grace period) +
  SIGKILL probes the prior process. Fail-safe on no-prior-state, ESRCH
  (already dead), and other kill errors.

- `resumeHarness.mjs` calls `terminatePreviousHarness(identity)` BEFORE
  the adapter dispatch (gated to non-osascript adapters since osascript
  has no leak surface). After successful spawn, `spawnAsync` records the
  PID via `recordHarnessProcess`. Wired uniformly for both `claude-cli`
  and `antigravity-cli` branches — single point of cleanup discipline.

- Six new harnessLifecycle unit tests via mock-bin pattern: no-prior-state
  fail-safe, persistence roundtrip, stale-dead-PID reaping with state-clear,
  live-spawned-process termination (real SIGTERM observed), state-file
  overwrite-on-record, identity sanitization for path-traversal prevention.

- Two new resumeHarness integration tests: claude-cli spawn records PID
  in state-file, stale dead PID is reaped before fresh spawn proceeds.

State-file location: `.neo-ai-data/harness-state/<sanitized-identity>.json`,
sibling to the inflightLock state directory. Both `wake-daemon/` and
`harness-state/` are per-identity primitive substrate.

Test evidence: harnessLifecycle 6 passed; resumeHarness 13 passed / 2 skipped
(live-host opt-in); checkSunsetted 11 passed. No downstream regression.

Cross-references:
- Sibling primitive: inflightLock.mjs (#10683) — per-identity data-layer mutex
- Companion forensic: learn/agentos/incidents/2026-05-04-runaway-spawn-pattern.md
- Cross-adapter beneficiary: antigravity-cli adapter (#10680) gets cleanup retroactively

* feat(skills): epic-resolution skill + Substrate Evidence Ladder graduation (#10698)

Discussion #10697 graduation artifact. Mental model per @tobiu:
"turn friction into gold" — convert the friction surfaced by PR #10696
/ issue #10677 cycle (rubber-stamp review missed substrate-truth violation,
mock-bin tests promoted to live-substrate proof, broadcast "components
look solid" treated as closeout) into durable workflow artifacts.

Five artifacts in this PR (small-map / deep-reference shape):

1. NEW SKILL `epic-resolution` (.agents/skills/epic-resolution/)
   Sibling to `epic-review`. Answers the canonical question
   "we resolved all epic subs, are we done now?" with a structured
   matrix + verdict recommendation (RECOMMEND_CLOSE_COMPLETED /
   KEEP_OPEN / CREATE_MISSING_SUBS / RETIRE_OR_SUPERSEDE).

   - SKILL.md: lightweight router with YAML frontmatter
   - references/epic-resolution-workflow.md: heavy payload (triggers,
     concurrency guard via discipline-layer mutex on epic comments,
     matrix population, verdict logic, A2A coordination)
   - .claude/skills/epic-resolution: required symlink

   Verdict authority parallel to §0 Invariant 1: skill produces
   RECOMMENDATIONS only; never autonomously closes the epic. Close-as-
   completed is reserved for the human pipeline authority.

   Concurrency guard: discipline-layer mutex via in-progress claim
   comment on the epic (not a new state-file primitive — lighter than
   the original ticket scope sketch). 30-min cooldown + optional
   primary-owner restriction.

2. NEW REFERENCE `learn/agentos/evidence-ladder.md`
   Authoritative L1-L4 ladder + sandbox-vs-achievable ceiling
   distinction + matrix schema + close-target Resolves rule.
   Cross-referenced by all three skill amendments.

3. PR-REVIEW SKILL AMENDMENT (assets/pr-review-template.md)
   Two new audit sections inserted after Close-Target Audit:
   - Evidence Audit: reviewer verifies PR body Evidence declaration
     against close-target ACs; flags evidence-class collapse
   - Source-of-Authority: reviewer citations of operator/peer
     authority must link source comment-id, A2A messageId, or be
     marked [paraphrase]. Sourced from feedback_peer_cited_authority_
     neutral_ask memory captured during this same session.

4. PULL-REQUEST SKILL AMENDMENT (references/pull-request-workflow.md)
   §9 PR Body Hygiene gets a 1-line Evidence declaration template:
   `Evidence: L<X> (...) → L<Y> required (...). Residual: AC<N> [#N].`
   Required for PRs whose close-target ACs include observable runtime
   effect on a surface the CI/sandbox cannot reach. Optional/N/A for
   PRs fully covered by unit tests / static contract.

5. IDEATION-SANDBOX SKILL AMENDMENT (narrowed per @neo-gpt's verify)
   §4 already supports [GRADUATED_TO_TICKET] marker. §5 prose was
   Epic-biased; rebalance to say graduation target may be Epic,
   standard ticket, or rare direct-PR depending scope. Empirical
   anchor: Discussion #10697 itself graduated to ticket #10698
   (single bounded artifact), not an Epic.

Plus AGENTS.md §21 entries:
- New row for epic-resolution skill trigger condition
- Updated row for epic-review noting sibling exit-pass
- Updated row for pull-request noting Evidence declaration
- Updated row for pr-review noting Evidence Audit + Source-of-
  Authority sections
- New Skill Trigger Pre-Flight bullet for epic-resolution

Verification per skill-authoring-guide.md:
- One SKILL.md
- YAML frontmatter (name, description, triggers)
- Heavy content (143 lines) in references/
- SKILL.md body has explicit view_file path
- .claude/skills/ symlink created

* fix(skills): epic-resolution link path + epic-review producer hook (#10698)

Cycle 1 review fixes per @neo-gpt's PR #10699 review (commentId
IC_kwDODSospM8AAAABBJ-Ngw).

1. Fix broken relative link in epic-resolution-workflow.md:44.
   Path was ../../../learn/... (3 levels up); should be
   ../../../../learn/... (4 levels up — file is at
   .agents/skills/epic-resolution/references/, repo root is 4 levels
   above). Other evidence-ladder references in the PR were already at
   the correct depth (verified: pr-review-template.md, pull-request-
   workflow.md).

2. Add epic-review Stage 3.1 producer hook (entry side of the closeout
   contract). The shared matrix schema requires both ends:
   epic-review SEEDS columns 1-3 (Parent AC, Required evidence, Owning
   sub(s)); epic-resolution RECONCILES columns 4-6 (Delivered PRs,
   Achieved evidence, Residual state). Without an explicit producer
   hook, epic-resolution claims to consume a matrix that nothing
   produces.

   Stage 3.1 added under Sub-Structure Coherence:
   - Trigger: when any of the epic's ACs describe observable runtime
     effect on a surface the CI/sandbox cannot reach (the same
     Evidence Ladder trigger applies)
   - Output: matrix posted as comment on epic OR amended into epic
     body as `## Closeout Matrix (entry-seeded)` section
   - Cross-reference to learn/agentos/evidence-ladder.md

3. Add epic-resolution row to epic-review §6 Relationship to Sibling
   Skills table — explicit declaration of the matrix-as-artifact
   contract between the two skills.

All 4 evidence-ladder relative links verified to resolve correctly:
- .agents/skills/pr-review/assets/pr-review-template.md
- .agents/skills/epic-resolution/references/epic-resolution-workflow.md
- .agents/skills/pull-request/references/pull-request-workflow.md
- .agents/skills/epic-review/references/epic-review-workflow.md

- 2026-05-04T17:20:47Z @tobiu referenced in commit `255f9ef` - "feat(ai): claude-cli adapter for Claude Desktop terminal-restart (#10677) (#10696)

* feat(ai): claude-cli adapter for Claude Desktop terminal-restart (#10677)

Switches the claude-desktop HARNESS_REGISTRY entry from the legacy Cmd+N
osascript adapter to the substrate-correct claude-cli adapter using the
embedded Claude Code CLI's --session-id <uuid> primitive.

Empirically validated in #10677 research-phase comment IC_kwDODSospM8AAAABBJDmZg:
the embedded `claude` CLI accepts --session-id with UUID validation
(rejects non-UUIDs at the boundary), providing harness-layer enforcement
of fresh sessionId per recovery invocation. This is the substrate-correct
primitive that #10627's prompt-layer plumbing tried (and failed
structurally) to achieve.

Implementation:
- New resolveClaudeCliPath() helper: env-var override (CLAUDE_CLI_PATH)
  for test-mock injection per #10681 discipline + dynamic version
  resolution (~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude-code/<version>)
  using numeric-aware sort so 2.1.121 ranks above 2.1.99
- HARNESS_REGISTRY claude-desktop: { adapter: 'claude-cli' }
- New adapter branch: generates fresh UUID via crypto.randomUUID(),
  spawns CLI with --session-id <uuid> + payload as positional args
- Fail-loud on CLI-not-found; ESC-as-rejection lock-clear path applies

Tests:
- New "Claude CLI: adapter executes --session-id <uuid> <payload>"
  using mock-bin pattern (parallel to Gemini's antigravity-cli test)
- New "Claude CLI: spawner generates fresh UUID per recovery invocation
  (no static reuse)" — verifies the per-recovery isolation property
- Updated existing tests: HARNESS_REGISTRY shape assertion + Q1b
  multi-harness test now expect claude-cli adapter
- ESC-as-rejection lock-clear test refactored to use fake claude binary
  (was fake osascript) since claude-desktop no longer routes osascript
- Verify-effect static-check tightened to set_session_id( call shape
  rather than substring (allows JSDoc references)

Tests: 14 passed / 2 skipped (live-host opt-in). Downstream regression
on checkSunsetted/inflightLock/swarm-heartbeat/idleOutNudge specs:
30 passed, no regression.

The legacy osascript adapter dispatch (Cmd+N keystroke) is preserved
in the script as fallback infrastructure; no production identity routes
there now. AC5 runtime verify-effect (pre/post sessionId mismatch via
real MCP roundtrip) remains gated on operator-controlled handoff window.

* fix(ai): drop --session-id plumbing from claude-cli adapter (#10677)

Substrate-truth course-correction. The original PR introduced
`claude --session-id <uuid> <payload>` invocation, claiming "harness-layer
fresh-sessionId enforcement at the CLI boundary." That framing reintroduced
exactly the spawner-side sessionId management that harness restart was
meant to eliminate.

The architectural goal of harness restart is precisely that fresh process
= fresh MCP client connection = fresh `currentSessionId` BY CONSTRUCTION.
No spawner-side enforcement needed because there is no shared state to
manage across the boundary. `--session-id` is the CLI's *resume* flag —
the OPPOSITE of what recovery needs.

Changes:
- Drop `randomUUID` import from resumeHarness.mjs.
- claude-cli dispatch now passes `[payload]` only (no flag).
- Update adapter doc-comment + HARNESS_REGISTRY comment to anchor the
  substrate-truth: fresh process = fresh session by construction.
- Spec test renamed/restructured to assert NO `--session-id` flag, single-
  arg payload. Drop the "fresh UUID per recovery" test (whole premise was
  invalidated — there is no UUID at all).
- Static config-check test comment updated.

Empirical verification of the spawn shape (Claude Desktop Tab 3 vs
terminal-attached CLI session) is still pending — captured as @todo on
the adapter. Either shape satisfies the substrate goal; the operator
surface differs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <claude@anthropic.com>

* feat(ai): cross-adapter harness-process cleanup primitive (#10677, #10696 review point 2)

Per @neo-gemini-3-1-pro's PR review (#10696) escalated by @tobiu: the new
CLI-based adapters (claude-cli, antigravity-cli) spawn distinct processes
on each sunset restart. Without explicit cleanup of the prior harness
instance, repeated sunsets accumulate stale processes and orphan windows.
The legacy osascript Cmd+N adapter didn't have this surface (Cmd+N spawned
a new chat WITHIN the same app instance) but the CLI-based replacements DO.

This PR adds the cross-adapter cleanup primitive:

- New `ai/scripts/harnessLifecycle.mjs` module owns the per-identity state
  file format and termination logic. `recordHarnessProcess` persists the
  spawned PID; `terminatePreviousHarness` SIGTERMs (with grace period) +
  SIGKILL probes the prior process. Fail-safe on no-prior-state, ESRCH
  (already dead), and other kill errors.

- `resumeHarness.mjs` calls `terminatePreviousHarness(identity)` BEFORE
  the adapter dispatch (gated to non-osascript adapters since osascript
  has no leak surface). After successful spawn, `spawnAsync` records the
  PID via `recordHarnessProcess`. Wired uniformly for both `claude-cli`
  and `antigravity-cli` branches — single point of cleanup discipline.

- Six new harnessLifecycle unit tests via mock-bin pattern: no-prior-state
  fail-safe, persistence roundtrip, stale-dead-PID reaping with state-clear,
  live-spawned-process termination (real SIGTERM observed), state-file
  overwrite-on-record, identity sanitization for path-traversal prevention.

- Two new resumeHarness integration tests: claude-cli spawn records PID
  in state-file, stale dead PID is reaped before fresh spawn proceeds.

State-file location: `.neo-ai-data/harness-state/<sanitized-identity>.json`,
sibling to the inflightLock state directory. Both `wake-daemon/` and
`harness-state/` are per-identity primitive substrate.

Test evidence: harnessLifecycle 6 passed; resumeHarness 13 passed / 2 skipped
(live-host opt-in); checkSunsetted 11 passed. No downstream regression.

Cross-references:
- Sibling primitive: inflightLock.mjs (#10683) — per-identity data-layer mutex
- Companion forensic: learn/agentos/incidents/2026-05-04-runaway-spawn-pattern.md
- Cross-adapter beneficiary: antigravity-cli adapter (#10680) gets cleanup retroactively
